description: Needle-in-a-haystack routing fixture. 20 software domains × 5 probe + 5 adversarial queries. Centroids are computed at test time from centroid_texts via EmbeddingService.

domains.domain_label: JWT authentication and session tokens
centroid_texts: JWT token generation, validation, and expiry using PyJWT library
Bearer token authentication middleware verifying HMAC signatures
User login flow: validate credentials, issue access token and refresh token
Session cookie management, token revocation, and logout endpoint
OAuth2 password grant flow with scope-based authorization claims
probe_queries: How does JWT token validation work in the middleware?
Where is the user login endpoint and how does it issue tokens?
How are refresh tokens rotated and revoked on logout?
What HMAC algorithm is used to sign the bearer tokens?
How does the session cookie get set after successful authentication?
adversarial_queries: How does the API verify the identity of an incoming request?
Where are user passwords hashed and compared during sign-in?
How does the system handle expired access credentials?
What happens when a user sends an invalid authorization header?
How is the current logged-in user retrieved inside a request handler?

domains.domain_label: SQLAlchemy ORM models and database relationships
centroid_texts: SQLAlchemy declarative Base, Column types, and relationship() foreign keys
ORM model definitions: User, Order, Product with one-to-many and many-to-many
SQLAlchemy session management, lazy loading, and eager loading with joinedload
Database query building with filter(), order_by(), limit(), and offset()
Composite indexes, unique constraints, and check constraints in ORM models
probe_queries: How is the User model defined with its foreign key relationships?
What SQLAlchemy session strategy is used for lazy vs eager loading?
How do I add a composite index to an ORM model?
Where are the many-to-many association tables declared?
How does the query filter users by their active status?
adversarial_queries: How does SQLAlchemy load model instances from database tables using session queries?
Where is the database connection pool configured?
How does the app handle duplicate key violations when inserting rows?
What tables are defined in the persistence layer?
How are one-to-many relationships traversed in queries?

domains.domain_label: FastAPI HTTP route handlers and request parsing
centroid_texts: FastAPI router, path parameters, query parameters, and response models
HTTP POST and GET endpoint definitions with Pydantic request body validation
Dependency injection in FastAPI: Depends(), request headers, path operations
HTTP status codes, error responses, and HTTPException in route handlers
API versioning with router prefix, tags, and OpenAPI schema generation
probe_queries: How is the POST /users endpoint defined and what does it validate?
Where are query parameters parsed and validated in the route handlers?
How does FastAPI inject dependencies into route functions?
What response model is returned by the GET /items/{id} endpoint?
How are HTTP 422 validation errors returned to the client?
adversarial_queries: How does the API handle an incoming HTTP request?
Where is request body deserialization handled?
How are URL path segments extracted for processing?
What happens when a required request parameter is missing?
How is the API documentation schema generated from the code?

domains.domain_label: React UI component rendering and state management
centroid_texts: React functional components, JSX rendering, props drilling, and children
useState, useEffect, useCallback hooks for component state and side effects
Redux store, actions, reducers, and useSelector/useDispatch hooks
React component lifecycle: mount, update, unmount cleanup in useEffect
Conditional rendering, list rendering with map() and key props in React
probe_queries: How does the UserCard component receive and render its props?
Where is the useState hook used to manage form input state?
How does the Redux store update when an action is dispatched?
What does the useEffect cleanup function do on component unmount?
How are list items rendered with unique key props in the component?
adversarial_queries: How is dynamic content displayed in the browser interface?
Where is the application's global state managed?
How does a button click trigger a data fetch and re-render?
What happens to subscriptions when a component is removed from the DOM?
How are child components passed data from their parent?

domains.domain_label: Redis caching layer and cache invalidation
centroid_texts: Redis SET, GET, DEL with TTL expiry for application-level caching
Cache-aside pattern: read-through and write-through with Redis client
Cache key namespacing, cache stampede prevention with distributed locks
Redis pipeline batching, connection pooling, and serialization with pickle/JSON
Cache invalidation strategies: event-based purge, TTL sweep, and LRU eviction
probe_queries: How is the Redis cache populated and what TTL is set on each key?
Where does the cache-aside pattern fetch from the source on a cache miss?
How are cache keys namespaced to prevent collisions between services?
What serialization format is used when storing objects in Redis?
How is the cache invalidated when the underlying database record changes?
adversarial_queries: How does the system avoid hitting the database on every request?
Where is frequently accessed data stored to reduce latency?
How does the app handle a cold cache when the service restarts?
What happens when a cached value becomes stale after an update?
How is in-memory data storage managed for high-traffic endpoints?

domains.domain_label: Celery background task queue and async job workers
centroid_texts: Celery task definitions with @app.task decorator, delay() and apply_async()
Celery beat periodic tasks, crontab schedule, and task routing to queues
Celery worker process management, concurrency, prefetch, and task retries
Task result backend: storing and retrieving Celery task results from Redis
Celery chord, chain, and group primitives for task workflow composition
probe_queries: How is the send_email Celery task defined and invoked asynchronously?
Where is the periodic task schedule configured in Celery beat?
How does the worker retry a failed task with exponential backoff?
What result backend stores the return value of a completed Celery task?
How is a chord used to aggregate results from a group of parallel tasks?
adversarial_queries: How does the application offload slow operations to background Celery workers?
Where are jobs enqueued for deferred processing outside the request cycle?
How does the system ensure a background job is retried after failure?
What happens when a worker process crashes mid-task?
How are scheduled recurring jobs defined and registered in the system?

domains.domain_label: AWS S3 file upload and blob storage operations
centroid_texts: AWS S3 bucket operations: PutObject, GetObject, DeleteObject with boto3
Presigned URL generation for direct browser-to-S3 multipart file uploads
S3 bucket policy, IAM role permissions, and server-side encryption at rest
S3 object metadata, content-type headers, and versioning configuration
S3 event notifications triggering Lambda on object creation or deletion
probe_queries: How is a presigned URL generated for direct S3 file upload from the browser?
Where is the boto3 S3 client configured with credentials and bucket name?
How does server-side encryption get applied to uploaded S3 objects?
What S3 event triggers the processing Lambda when a file is uploaded?
How is multipart upload assembled and completed for large files?
adversarial_queries: How does the system handle large binary file uploads from users?
Where are user-uploaded assets stored and retrieved in the cloud?
How are temporary download links generated for private files?
What permissions are required to read and write stored objects?
How does the application manage uploaded document storage and retrieval?

domains.domain_label: Elasticsearch full-text search indexing and queries
centroid_texts: Elasticsearch index mapping, analyzers, and full-text search with match queries
Elasticsearch bool query: must, should, filter, and must_not clauses
Index document ingestion, bulk indexing, and reindexing pipelines
Elasticsearch aggregations: terms, histogram, date_histogram, and nested
Search relevance scoring with BM25, function_score, and field boosting
probe_queries: How is the Elasticsearch index mapping defined for the product catalog?
Where is the bool query assembled with must and filter clauses?
How are documents bulk-indexed into Elasticsearch during data ingestion?
What aggregation computes facets for the search results sidebar?
How is BM25 relevance tuned with field boosting for title vs description?
adversarial_queries: How does the application find records matching a keyword query?
Where is text tokenization and analysis configured for search?
How are search results ranked and sorted by relevance score?
What happens when a search query returns zero matching documents?
How is autocomplete or typeahead implemented for the search bar?

domains.domain_label: Stripe payment processing and webhook billing
centroid_texts: Stripe PaymentIntent creation, confirmation, and 3D Secure authentication
Stripe webhook endpoint: signature verification and event handling
Stripe subscription creation, plan changes, proration, and cancellation
Stripe customer portal, invoice generation, and failed payment retries
Stripe Connect platform payments, transfer splits, and payout schedules
probe_queries: How is a Stripe PaymentIntent created and confirmed on the server?
Where is the Stripe webhook signature verified before processing events?

adversarial_queries: How does the application handle a successful purchase transaction?
Where is the credit card charge initiated and confirmed?
How are recurring billing cycles managed for subscribers?
What callback is triggered when a payment is completed externally?

domains.domain_label: WebSocket server connection lifecycle and real-time messaging
centroid_texts: WebSocket server handshake, connection upgrade, and keep-alive ping/pong
Broadcasting messages to connected WebSocket clients in a channel room
WebSocket connection registry, client disconnection cleanup, and reconnect
Real-time event push over WebSocket: chat messages, notifications, live updates
WebSocket authentication via token query parameter on connection establishment
probe_queries: How is the WebSocket handshake performed and the connection upgraded?
Where does the server broadcast a message to all clients in a room?
How is a disconnected WebSocket client removed from the connection registry?
What event is sent over the WebSocket when a new chat message arrives?
How is the user authenticated when establishing the WebSocket connection?
adversarial_queries: How does the server push live updates to the browser without polling?
Where are persistent bidirectional connections between client and server managed?
How does the application notify connected users of a new event in real time?
What happens when a client's network drops and they attempt to reconnect?
How are real-time collaborative features implemented in the application?

domains.domain_label: GraphQL schema type definitions and resolvers
centroid_texts: GraphQL SDL schema: type Query, type Mutation, type definitions with fields
GraphQL resolver functions for nested types and data fetching with DataLoader
GraphQL mutations for create, update, delete operations with input types
GraphQL subscriptions for real-time data push over WebSocket protocol
GraphQL error handling, custom directives, and schema stitching
probe_queries: Where is the GraphQL type definition for the User query field?
How does the resolver fetch nested data and avoid N+1 queries with DataLoader?
How is a GraphQL mutation defined to create a new resource?
Where are GraphQL subscriptions set up for real-time data streaming?
How are custom GraphQL directives implemented and applied to fields?
adversarial_queries: How does the API expose data querying capabilities to clients?
Where are the schema definitions for the available API operations?
How does the GraphQL server resolve queries for nested related resource types?
What mechanism does the client use to fetch only the fields it needs?
How are complex data transformation and composition queries handled?

domains.domain_label: pytest test fixtures, mocks, and test infrastructure
centroid_texts: pytest fixtures with scope, tmp_path, monkeypatch, and conftest.py
unittest.mock: MagicMock, patch decorator, side_effect, and assert_called_with
pytest parametrize for table-driven tests and hypothesis property-based testing
Pytest-asyncio async test functions with event loop and AsyncMock
Test isolation: database transactions rollback, factory_boy fixtures, faker data
probe_queries: How is the database fixture set up to rollback after each test?
Where is monkeypatch used to replace an external service call in tests?
How does pytest.mark.parametrize generate multiple test cases from a table?
How is AsyncMock configured to simulate an async API call in tests?
Where does conftest.py define shared fixtures for the test suite?
adversarial_queries: How does the test suite verify the correctness of the application logic?
Where are fake objects used to isolate units under test?
How are test cases structured to cover edge cases and error paths?
What setup and teardown routines run before and after each test function?
How does the test framework handle flaky network calls in unit tests?

domains.domain_label: Docker containerization and Kubernetes deployment
centroid_texts: Dockerfile: multi-stage build, base image selection, COPY, RUN, ENTRYPOINT
Docker Compose services, volumes, networks, environment variables, and healthchecks
Kubernetes Deployment manifest: replicas, rolling update strategy, resource limits
Kubernetes Service, Ingress, ConfigMap, and Secret resource definitions
Helm chart templating, values.yaml overrides, and chart dependency management
probe_queries: How is the multi-stage Dockerfile structured to minimise the final image size?
Where are environment variables injected into the Docker Compose service?
How does the Kubernetes Deployment define rolling update and rollback strategy?

How is a Helm chart values override used to deploy to production vs staging?
adversarial_queries: How is the application packaged and deployed to a server environment?
Where is the container image built and pushed to a registry?
How are service dependencies started and linked in the local dev environment?
How does the orchestration system restart a crashed application instance?
Where is infrastructure-as-code used to describe the deployment topology?

domains.domain_label: Structured logging, observability, and log configuration
centroid_texts: structlog JSON logging with bound context: request_id, user_id, component name
Python logging configuration: handlers, formatters, log levels, and propagation
Log correlation IDs for distributed tracing across microservice boundaries
Log rotation, log shipping to ELK stack, and retention policy configuration
Metrics and counters emitted alongside log events for observability dashboards
probe_queries: How is a structlog logger bound with request_id and component name context?
Where is the Python logging configuration set up for the application?
How is a correlation ID injected into log records across service calls?
What log rotation and shipping configuration sends logs to the ELK stack?
How are counters and metrics emitted alongside structured log events?
adversarial_queries: How does the application record what happened during a request for debugging?
Where is contextual information attached to diagnostic output?
How are log messages from different services correlated in production?
What infrastructure collects and indexes application logs centrally?
How does the system track the frequency of errors and warnings over time?

domains.domain_label: API rate limiting and request throttling middleware
centroid_texts: Token bucket rate limiter: refill rate, burst capacity, and per-client key
Sliding window request counter in Redis for per-IP and per-API-key throttling
Rate limit HTTP response headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, Retry-After
Rate limiting middleware: FastAPI/Flask decorator checking quota before handler
Distributed rate limiting across multiple API server instances using Redis atomic ops
probe_queries: How is the token bucket algorithm implemented for per-client rate limiting?
Where does the sliding window counter in Redis enforce the request quota?
What HTTP headers communicate the rate limit status to API consumers?
How does the rate limiting middleware intercept requests before the route handler?
How is rate limiting kept consistent across multiple load-balanced API instances?
adversarial_queries: How does the API protect itself from being overwhelmed by too many requests?
Where is the per-user request quota enforced in the request pipeline?
How does the server respond when a client exceeds the allowed request rate?
What mechanism prevents a single client from monopolising API capacity?
How is abuse prevention implemented for high-frequency API consumers?

domains.domain_label: Application configuration loading from env files and TOML
centroid_texts: Pydantic BaseSettings loading configuration from environment variables and .env file
TOML configuration file parsing with tomllib and typed dataclass defaults
Secret management: injecting API keys from environment without hardcoding
Configuration validation at startup: required fields, type coercion, range checks
Environment-specific config profiles: development, staging, and production overrides
probe_queries: How does Pydantic BaseSettings load the DATABASE_URL from the environment?
Where is the TOML config file parsed and mapped to typed dataclass fields?

adversarial_queries: How does the application read its operational parameters at startup?
Where are environment-specific settings defined and loaded?
How does the system handle a missing required configuration value?

Email bounce handling, unsubscribe links, and suppression list management
Async email delivery queue: retry on SMTP timeout, dead-letter queue for failures

domains.domain_label: SSH key management and server provisioning with Ansible

domains.domain_label: Tree-sitter AST parsing and source code analysis
centroid_texts: Tree-sitter parser for Python: loading language grammar, parsing source bytes
AST node traversal: cursor.goto_first_child(), node.type, node.text extraction
Extracting function definitions, class bodies, and docstrings from syntax tree
Tree-sitter query patterns: S-expression syntax for matching named AST nodes
Incremental parsing with tree-sitter: applying edits to re-parse changed regions
